message:
fix(web): move active:scale-95 to base styles in QuoteBankViewer tags

Previously, active:scale-95 was only applied to unselected tag buttons.
Now it's in base styles so selected buttons also get tap feedback when
toggled off, matching SpotlightSearch CategoryPill behavior.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.4","title":"Protocol: Agent Mail thread contract (message types + ack semantics)","description":"# Goal
Standardize how sessions use Agent Mail so orchestration is deterministic.

This is critical in a **no vendor API** architecture because sessions are *messages + compilation*:
- agents run in terminal cockpits (often via `ntm`)
- they post results back to Agent Mail threads

So: we need a contract the compiler can rely on.

---

# Define
- thread id conventions (what counts as a session thread)
- message types (kickoff, role response/delta, compiled artifact, critique/round-2)
- subject conventions
- required body sections
- ack policy (when required, how to track)

---

# Acceptance
- A written spec exists for message types + required fields.
- The CLI and web can rely on these conventions.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.4.1","title":"Specify thread + subject conventions","description":"# Goal
Define conventions for:
- thread IDs (session IDs)
- message subjects (prefixing, role tags)

This is the ergonomic layer that makes a mailbox/thread list intelligible at a glance.

---

# Thread ID convention (recommended)
- Engineering work: use the Beads ID as the Agent Mail `thread_id`.
- Research sessions: use `RS-YYYYMMDD-short-slug` (stable, human-readable).

---

# Subject convention (recommended)
Use short, machine-detectable prefixes:
- KICKOFF: …
- DELTA[role]: …  (e.g., DELTA[gpt], DELTA[opus], DELTA[gemini])
- COMPILED: …
- CRITIQUE: …

---

# Acceptance
- A human can look at an inbox and understand what happened in a thread instantly.
- An orchestrator can classify messages without brittle heuristics.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.6","title":"Repo hygiene: ignore generated dirs (node_modules, coverage, playwright)","description":"# Goal
Prevent accidental commits and tooling noise from generated directories.

# Background
- This repo currently has a minimal `.gitignore` and generated directories commonly appear in working trees (`node_modules`, Playwright reports, coverage, Next build output, etc.).
- This increases risk of accidentally committing artifacts and also makes tools like `ubs` noisy when scanning the repo.

# Current risk
`git ls-files` shows at least one tracked file under `node_modules/` (likely a generated Vitest cache file).

Per AGENTS.md, removing it from git requires a destructive git operation; I need explicit user-provided approval before I can run or propose the exact command.

# Scope
- Expand `.gitignore` with common generated directories and files:
  - `node_modules/`
  - `apps/web/node_modules/`
  - `apps/web/.next/`
  - `apps/web/playwright-report/`
  - `apps/web/test-results/`
  - `apps/web/coverage/`
  - `dist/`
  - `*.tsbuildinfo`

# Acceptance criteria
- `.gitignore` updates prevent new generated artifacts from being staged by default.
- Follow-up is documented for cleaning the already-tracked `node_modules/` artifact (requires explicit approval).

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.2","title":"EPIC: Corpus Engine (parse/search/quote-bank/excerpts)","description":"# Goal
Make the corpus usable as a *tool*, not just a document:
- fast full-text search across the transcript + distillations
- stable anchors (`§n`) and excerpt composition
- a quote bank tagged by operators/primitives

# Why this matters

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.3","title":"EPIC: Agent Orchestration Layer (Agent Mail + artifact compiler)","description":"# Goal
Implement the multi-agent loop as an **orchestrator** that coordinates *operator-run* CLI agents via Agent Mail.

This layer does NOT call model APIs. Its job is:
- turn a kickoff (template + excerpt + optional memory) into role-specific prompts
- send prompts to agents via Agent Mail threads
- track acknowledgements + responses
- parse structured deltas
- compile/merge into canonical artifacts
- publish compiled artifacts back to the same thread

---

# Key constraint
Agents are humans-in-the-loop tools:
- Codex CLI / Claude Code / Gemini CLI run in terminal sessions (often in an `ntm` tmux cockpit).
- They communicate results back by posting messages to Agent Mail.

So: orchestration is *message passing + compilation*, not remote inference.

---

# The thread contract
- **Agent Mail `thread_id` is the join key**. Prefer using the Beads ID as the thread ID for engineering work.
- The orchestrator must be able to reconstruct session state from thread messages.

We need a lightweight message taxonomy (subjects or frontmatter) so compilation is deterministic:
- kickoff
- agent delta response
- compiled artifact
- critique round
- operator/admin notes

---

# Definition of done (for this epic)
- Kickoff sending is deterministic and role-aware.
- Thread status can be computed (who responded, latest artifact, missing acks).
- Compiler produces a canonical artifact that passes lint rules.
- Compiled artifact can be published back to the Agent Mail thread.
- Persistence (optional) is explicit, minimal-sprawl, and keyed by thread id.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.3.1.1","title":"Agent Mail: document tool/resource contracts we rely on","description":"# Goal
Document the Agent Mail MCP contracts Brenner Bot relies on, including:
- which MCP tools we call
- which resources we read (and their URI templates)
- encoding/quoting pitfalls (e.g. URL-encode absolute paths in query params)
- fail-soft vs fail-closed behavior by context (public web vs lab vs CLI)

---

# Minimum inventory
Tools:
- ensure_project
- register_agent
- send_message
- fetch_inbox
- mark_message_read
- acknowledge_message

Resources (templates):
- resource://agents/{project_key}
- resource://thread/{thread_id}?project=...
- resource://inbox/{agent}?project=...&limit=...
- plus any views we use (ack-required, urgent-unread)

---

# Gotchas to capture
- When passing an absolute path as a query param value (e.g. project=/data/...): URL-encode it (project=%2Fdata%2F...).
- Some servers may respond via SSE; client should present actionable errors.

---

# Acceptance
- A written contract exists that a future contributor can implement against.
- All callers can share a small URI-builder helper with proper encoding.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.5","title":"EPIC: CLI/Binary (brenner)","description":"# Goal
Turn `brenner.ts` into a **single executable** that makes Brenner Bot usable from the terminal:
- corpus-first workflows (search/read/excerpt)
- session workflows (compose → send → fetch → compile → publish)
- Agent Mail tooling (agents/inbox/thread/ack)
- optional memory augmentation via `cass-memory (cm)`

Constraint: Bun-only, and should compile to a single executable.

---

# How this fits the no-API architecture
The CLI is the *operator’s control surface* for a cockpit-first system:
- It coordinates **messages** (Agent Mail threads), not model inference.
- It can be used alongside `ntm` to run Codex/Claude/Gemini CLIs in parallel.

Recommended convention:
- For engineering work, use the Beads ID as the Agent Mail `thread_id`.
